rationale: The signal set is heavily dominated by Google autocomplete (95 suggestions) with broad, generic search intent: care, husbandry, prices, and listings. YouTube contains mostly listicle-style content ("Top 12 Morphs," "Ultimate Guide") and some valuable breeding controversy (cappuccino debates, breeding warnings). However, there is no forum discussion, no trending topics, and no recent breeder insights beyond a sale announcement. The morph-specific autocomplete signals (cappuccino, lilly white, axanthic, soft scale) show reader interest, but Google AC alone cannot ground a claim. The cappuccino slug is already published. I found one viable angle: the YouTube evidence suggests genuine confusion and disagreement about cappuccino breeding ethics and super-cappuccino viability—a real mythbuster opportunity with forum-like heat in comments. The lilly white signals show identification confusion (multiple "how to tell" videos). The axanthic and soft scale signals are weaker—mostly price and availability searches. Given the bar is high and duplicate/care-guide rejections apply, I am emitting only one strong topic.
